text: The surgeon performs a urethrovesicular anastomosis during a laparoscopic radical prostatectomy, using a right-handed suture technique. The suture is placed at the six o'clock position with two monocryl stitches using a euro one needle, five by eight. The suture is inserted inside out into the urethra, demonstrating right-handed forward rotation bites.

The surgeon demonstrates suture techniques, using a left-hand bite from the eleven o'clock to five o'clock position and a right-hand bite from the three o'clock position. The non-braided monofilament monocryl suture is pulled through, followed by a reverse right-hand bite. The left-hand suture is placed from nine o'clock to three o'clock, and a simple right-hand suture is also placed from nine o'clock to three o'clock on the right side of the patient.
The surgeon uses a small needle to perform a square knot suture with a 5/8 circle Euro 1 monocryl needle, demonstrating the technique of utilizing the needle's curve for efficient suturing.
